summary: Iter16 verification of new AI Test Bench feature (per-model probes, /run/{model}, /run-all, /history, persistence). Backend pytest 6/6 PASS in 5.74s. Frontend Playwright PASS — 8 model cards render with correct data-testids, Run probes on gpt-4.1 updated card with avg ms + status icon, Probes(3) expanded with health/arithmetic/learnings rows, Run all refreshed all 8 cards and headline showed 'Passing 1/8' (expected — strict learnings probe).

design_issues.screen: Operator → AI Tests tab on fresh nav
issues: The 'Operator Quick Guide' onboarding tour modal pops over the tab content on first load. Doesn't block testid-based clicks (force=True bypasses), but a real operator has to dismiss it before they can interact with the cards visually. Not new to this iteration — same tour exists across the operator console.

critical_code_review_comments: ai_test_bench_ext.py:60-62 _provider_for raises HTTPException(400) for unknown ids — exercised cleanly via /run/not-a-real-model returning 400. Good.

ai_test_bench_ext.py:164-167 _run_one_model uses asyncio.gather(return_exceptions=False) for the per-probe fan-out. Each _run_probe already catches its own exceptions, so this is safe — but if a future refactor removes _run_probe's try/except, one bad probe would 500 the whole model run. Worth a comment in code or switching to return_exceptions=True defensively.

ai_test_bench_ext.py:241-261 run-all uses return_exceptions=True at the model level — exactly right. Exception-wrapped models surface as {pass:false, error:...} rather than 500-ing the batch. Verified empirically: /run-all completed all 8 models in ~3 seconds and 7 returned fail (learnings strict-match) but the endpoint still returned 200.

ai_test_bench_ext.py:127-157 _build_probes — distinctive-token regex r'\b([A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9-]{3,12})\b' picks the *first* 4-12 char token in the learning text. The probe asks the model to 'Quote the most important word from this rule, verbatim' but pass_check is satisfied if the first matched token (not necessarily the 'most important' word) appears anywhere in the reply. This is why ~7/8 models 'fail' the learnings probe: the token is often a stopword like 'INSIDE' / 'When' that the model doesn't echo. Not a bug, but the probe's title 'learnings' over-promises — consider renaming to 'regression-keyword-echo' or scoring more loosely (substring + tokenisation).

ai_test_bench_ext.py:201-209 list_models uses an in-Python dedupe sorted by created_at desc over a 200-row window. Fine at current scale (8 models, infrequent runs). If runs become hourly across many models the 200-row window could miss the most recent run for an inactive model — easy fix later via aggregation pipeline ({$sort}, {$group:{_id:'$model',doc:{$first:'$$ROOT'}}}).
AITestBenchTab.jsx:121 grid uses sm/lg/xl breakpoints; on Tab (768px) it falls to sm:grid-cols-2 (4 rows of 2). Looks fine in screenshot.

AITestBenchTab.jsx:46 toast colour uses data.pass branch — for partial passes (arithmetic ok, learnings fail) it shows the *error* variant. Matches the strict top-level pass semantics documented in the request, but a user may prefer 'partial' / amber styling when only the regression probe fails.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — all 4 requested UI flows verified (cards render, Run probes single, Probes expand, Run all + headline)

test_credentials: Operator: rac.investments.swe@gmail.com / REDACTED — boots without TOTP, lands on /setup-2fa but /operator works via tbc_session cookie.
seed_data_creation: Three ai_model_tests docs persisted during testing (one from /run/gpt-4.1, plus eight from /run-all). Not cleaned up by the tests — they're intentionally kept as historical data the operator can browse. They are correctly namespaced in the ai_model_tests collection and do not pollute any other collection.
context_for_next_testing_agent: 1) Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_iter16_ai_test_bench.py — run `cd /app/backend && pytest tests/test_iter16_ai_test_bench.py -v` (~6s with hot LLM cache, <60s cold). Tests are sequenced via pytest.<attr> stash — keep TestRunSingleModel and TestHistory in the same module run. 2) /api/operator/ai-tests endpoints are operator-only (Depends(get_current_operator)) — login flow identical to iter15 (POST /api/auth/login with email+password returns token, set Authorization Bearer). 3) /run-all empirically completes in ~3 seconds — parallel asyncio.gather works correctly. 4) Top-level `pass` is intentionally strict (ALL probes must pass). With at least one active learning seeded, expect 'pass=false' for most models because the learnings keyword echo is harsh. This is BY DESIGN — do not flag as a bug. 5) Frontend tab is registered at /operator?tab=ai-tests (deep link works). Use data-testid='ai-test-model-<id>' for cards, 'ai-test-run-<id>' for per-model run, 'ai-tests-run-all' for batch, 'ai-test-probe-<modelid>-<probename>' for expanded probe rows.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs found. All 6 backend tests and all UI flows pass. The 7/8 'fail' top-level outcome from /run-all is the documented strict-regression behaviour, not a defect.
